text: Patient presents with SOB, DOE, and JVD. CXR shows cardiomegaly with bilateral pleural effusions. ICD-10: I50.9, J81.1. Continue furosemide 40mg IV BID, monitor I/Os and daily weights.
ATIS ZULU: Wind 28015G25KT, CAVOK, QNH 1012, TEMPO 1500 BR. Landing and departing runway 26R. Advise on initial contact.
SITREP: 3rd Bn, 187th INF, OPORD FRAGO 01-24. Objective NASHVILLE SECURED at 1430Z. 2x KIA, 5x WIA (all ambulatory). Enemy contact: 1x KIA, 3x EKIA. IED strike at grid NJ 3456 7890, route BLUE.

commit 7f3e5a1b2c8d9e0f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b9c0d1e2f
Author: johndoe <johndoe@kernel.org>
Date: Mon Mar 18 14:32:51 2024 -0400

    mm: fix page fault scalability regression
    
    Revert 'mm: use raw_cpu_ptr for vmstat stats' which introduced
    a cacheline bouncing issue on NUMA systems with >64 CPUs.
    
    Signed-off-by: John Doe <johndoe@kernel.org>

A composition of matter comprising: (a) a first nucleic acid sequence encoding a polypeptide having at least 95% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 1; (b) a second nucleic acid sequence operably linked to a heterologous promoter; and (c) a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, wherein said polypeptide specifically binds to TNF-alpha.

What's the difference between a credit default swap and a total return swap in terms of counterparty risk? Also, how does the ISDA master agreement handle netting in a bankruptcy scenario?

The patient's medication list includes: Metformin 1000mg PO BID, Lisinopril 10mg PO QD, Atorvastatin 20mg PO QHS, and Insulin Glargine 30 units SC QHS. Recent HbA1c: 8.2%. Plan: titrate Glargine to 35 units, add Jardiance 10mg PO QD.

SYNTHESIS OF 2-AMINO-4-(4-FLUOROPHENYL)-6-METHYL-5-OXO-5,6-DIHYDRO-4H-PYRANO[3,2-C]QUINOLINE-3-CARBONITRILE: A mixture of 4-fluoro benzaldehyde (10 mmol), malononitrile (10 mmol), and 4-hydroxy-6-methyl-2H-pyrano[3,2-c]quinolin-5(6H)-one (10 mmol) in ethanol (50 mL) containing piperidine (0.5 mL) was refluxed for 4 h.

OPORD 23-042: TF 1-37 AR conducts deliberate attack NLT 0600Z to seize OBJ BRAVO. Scheme of maneuver: A/1-37 fixes from the north, B/1-37 breaches obstacle at PL DOG, C/1-37 assaults through breach. FSCM: Priority of fires to B/1-37. CAS: 2x F-16s on station at 0530Z.

Can you parse this patent claim: A method for modulating the expression of a target gene in a cell comprising contacting said cell with an effective amount of a double-stranded ribonucleic acid molecule, wherein said double-stranded ribonucleic acid molecule comprises a sense strand and an antisense strand, each strand being between 18 and 30 nucleotides in length, and wherein said antisense strand comprises a region of complementarity to said target gene, and further wherein said double-stranded ribonucleic acid molecule is conjugated to a targeting ligand.

SOAP note: 58 yo M with PMHx of HTN, DM2 presents with pleuritic CP. Vitals: BP 148/92, HR 102, RR 18, O2 sat 94% RA. CXR: LLL infiltrate. Imp: CAP. Plan: Augmentin 875/125 BID x7d, follow-up in 2 wks. Is this appropriate?

SITREP: BLUF: OP CORAL REEF complete. TF RED secured OBJ DOGWOOD at 0430Z. KIA: 2, WIA: 5 (all non-critical). Equipment damage: 1x M-ATV minor IED damage. ENY KIA est. 12. Current posture: defensive, awaiting LOGPAC. Any questions?
